notes: Reproduced: SKBitmap.ScalePixels with SKCubicResampler.Mitchell produces 12% larger WebP output (12,252 bytes) compared to v2.88.9 SKFilterQuality.High (10,962 bytes) when downscaling a 1440x720 image to 480x240. This is because Skia's old SKFilterQuality.High internally switched to bilinear+mipmaps for downscaling, but the new SKSamplingOptions(SKCubicResampler.Mitchell) always uses cubic. The workaround SKSamplingOptions(SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear) produces output (10,748 bytes) comparable to v2. Bug persists on latest stable 3.119.2 and main branch. This appears to be a documentation/migration gap rather than a code defect — the v2-to-v3 migration guidance should clarify that SKFilterQuality.High maps to different sampling options depending on scale direction.

reproductionSteps.description: Create console repro project with SkiaSharp 2.88.9 and download reporter's test image
reproductionSteps.command: mkdir -p /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3159/v2 && cd /tmp/skiasharp/repro/3159/v2 && dotnet new console -n Repro --framework net8.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.9 && curl -sL -o large.png https://ptdt.azureedge.net/data/test/large.png
reproductionSteps.output: Package 'SkiaSharp' version '2.88.9' added. Image: PNG 1440x720 RGBA, 101032 bytes.

versionResults.notes: Baseline: SKFilterQuality.High produced 10,962 byte WebP output. This is the expected/good behavior.
Mitchell cubic produced 12,252 bytes (12% larger than v2). Linear+Mipmap workaround produced 10,748 bytes (comparable to v2).
Identical to 3.116.1: cubic=12,252 bytes, linear+mipmap=10,748 bytes. Bug persists on latest stable.
Identical to NuGet releases: cubic=12,252 bytes, linear+mipmap=10,748 bytes. Bug persists on main branch.

errorMessages.primaryError: SKBitmap.ScalePixels with SKCubicResampler.Mitchell produces 12% larger and lower quality WebP output when downscaling compared to v2.88.9 SKFilterQuality.High

actionability.reason: The behavior is caused by a leaky abstraction in Skia's old SKFilterQuality API — it internally switched to bilinear+mipmaps for downscaling, but this was never documented. The v3 migration to explicit SKSamplingOptions exposed this hidden behavior. A community member has already identified the root cause and workaround. This could be addressed with better migration documentation or a helper method that auto-selects sampling based on scale direction.

actions.comment:
Thanks for reporting this! We've confirmed the behavior difference when downsizing images between v2 and v3.

**Root cause:** In v2, `SKFilterQuality.High` internally switched to bilinear + mipmaps when *downscaling* (this was an undocumented Skia optimization). The v3 migration to `SKSamplingOptions(SKCubicResampler.Mitchell)` always uses cubic resampling regardless of scale direction, which produces larger/lower-quality results specifically for downscaling.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Sampling Method | WebP Size (q75) |
|---------|----------------|------------------|
| 2.88.9 | `SKFilterQuality.High` | 10,962 bytes |
| 3.116.1 | `SKCubicResampler.Mitchell` | 12,252 bytes (+12%) |
| 3.116.1 | `SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear` | 10,748 bytes |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | `SKCubicResampler.Mitchell` | 12,252 bytes (+12%) |

**Workaround:** As @gnattu noted, use different sampling options based on scale direction:
```csharp
bool isDownscaling = targetWidth < sourceWidth || targetHeight < sourceHeight;
var samplingOptions = isDownscaling
    ? new SKSamplingOptions(SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear)
    : new SKSamplingOptions(SKCubicResampler.Mitchell);
bitmapSrc.ScalePixels(bitmapDst, samplingOptions);
```

This produces output comparable to v2's `SKFilterQuality.High` for both up- and downscaling.

actions.description: Add area label for SkiaSharp core

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! We've confirmed the behavior difference when downsizing images between v2 and v3.

**Root cause:** In v2, `SKFilterQuality.High` internally switched to bilinear + mipmaps when *downscaling* (this was an undocumented Skia optimization). The v3 migration to `SKSamplingOptions(SKCubicResampler.Mitchell)` always uses cubic resampling regardless of scale direction, which produces larger/lower-quality results specifically for downscaling.

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Sampling Method | WebP Size (q75) |
|---------|----------------|------------------|
| 2.88.9 | `SKFilterQuality.High` | 10,962 bytes |
| 3.116.1 | `SKCubicResampler.Mitchell` | 12,252 bytes (+12%) |
| 3.116.1 | `SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear` | 10,748 bytes |
| 3.119.2 (latest) | `SKCubicResampler.Mitchell` | 12,252 bytes (+12%) |

**Workaround:** As @gnattu noted, use different sampling options based on scale direction:
```csharp
bool isDownscaling = targetWidth < sourceWidth || targetHeight < sourceHeight;
var samplingOptions = isDownscaling
    ? new SKSamplingOptions(SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear)
    : new SKSamplingOptions(SKCubicResampler.Mitchell);
bitmapSrc.ScalePixels(bitmapDst, samplingOptions);
```

This produces output comparable to v2's `SKFilterQuality.High` for both up- and downscaling.

workarounds: Use SKSamplingOptions(SKFilterMode.Linear, SKMipmapMode.Linear) instead of SKCubicResampler.Mitchell when downscaling images

corrections.corrected: Behavior is technically reproduced as reported, but root cause is a leaky abstraction in upstream Skia's old SKFilterQuality API rather than a SkiaSharp defect. Assessment: working-as-designed with documentation gap.
